findings.problem: The tailgate negotiation runs as an external transcript for a long stretch (13 consecutive paragraphs) before re-entering Lena's interior at line 145. The chapter temporarily loses close-third pressure and reads flatter than the surrounding material.
findings.rewrite_direction: Insert focalized interior beats in the same exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:129, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:137, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:141. Keep them brief (1-2 sentences each) and specific to Lena (body sensation, memory flash, or self-assessment) so the Otis dialogue still drives the scene.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:119 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:143 contains at least 3 distinct Lena-internal beats (thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment), and no run longer than 5 consecutive paragraphs in that span is purely external action/dialogue. FAIL otherwise.

findings.problem: A recurring reaction template ("gave her a [adjective] look") appears three times and starts to feel mechanical rather than character-specific, flattening tension beats during key trust negotiations.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise reaction beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:31, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:197 so at least two of the three use different constructions tied to context (e.g., gesture, posture shift, or speech interruption) rather than the repeated visual-look formula.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, the exact construction pattern used at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:31 ("gave her a ... look") appears no more than once across those three target lines and each replacement beat conveys a distinct social pressure cue. FAIL if the same formula still appears 2+ times in that set.

summary: Chapter 10 lands its causal objective and boundary beats: quiet local texture, partial oral history, and a materially useful map that cleanly drives Chapter 11. Voice separation is strong (Lena/Beau/Otis), contraction use is natural for register, and tense/continuity checks are clean against style bible and continuity sheet. It fails craft/prose on two unresolved quality risks: a long interiority drop that temporarily screens the chapter into transcript mode, and a repeated reaction-beat sentence formula that reads as prose autopilot.
